title: Cloud AI agent that controls the glasses browser
description:
Add an AI-driven control mode to the remote dashboard. User types a natural-language instruction (e.g. 'find the next Back Anchor video and play it') and a cloud-hosted agent drives the glasses' browser via the existing HTTP API until done.

## Architecture sketch

- **Tools exposed to the agent** (already built into ControlServer): open URL, run JS, tap-element by selector, get DOM, get screenshot, refresh, back/forward, get title. All that's needed for a web-driving agent.
- **Agent runtime**: headless Claude Code (or a trimmed pi-ai agent loop) running on an always-on server. Candidates: Jacob's HMA Mac mini, Lightsail (3.216.129.34), or a small dedicated VM. Could also be a clawdbot skill.
- **Reach glasses from server**: glasses are on local WiFi so the server can't reach them directly. Options: (a) Tailscale node on glasses (likely infeasible on Android without root), (b) Tailscale Funnel on Mac mini + Mac mini relays to local WiFi, (c) glasses open a WebSocket to the server (reverse tunnel — easiest), (d) ngrok/cloudflared tunnel started by a Mac helper.
- **User input channel**: (1) text box on the existing iPhone dashboard, (2) voice via Whisper, (3) Slack/Telegram message forwarded by clawdbot.
- **Guardrails**: allowlist of domains, max N actions per task, screenshot diff + 'continue?' prompt for ambiguous state.

## MVP
1. Add 'AI assistant' text box to dashboard.
2. Dashboard POSTs instruction + current URL to a new /ai endpoint (glasses-local).
3. /ai forwards to a cloud agent URL (configurable), passing the URL + instruction.
4. Agent loops: call Anthropic API with tool use, invoke one of our HTTP endpoints per tool call, re-screenshot, iterate until done.
5. Agent emits a final message rendered in the dashboard.

## Dependencies
- rn-be7 (epic)
- blocked-by: exposing glasses HTTP to the cloud agent (separate ticket worth breaking out)

## Why this is interesting
Current dashboard is mechanical ('tap first Wistia iframe'). A real agent handles 'play the next lesson in Module 1' where it has to inspect the DOM, decide which element, handle auth redirects, handle Wistia's overlay, etc. That's exactly what Claude Code does on a codebase — same loop, different tools.

title: Build rn CLI on Mac side
description:
Python CLI at ~/code/RayNeoTools/cli/rn. Commands:
- rn open URL (launch browser app with VIEW intent)
- rn type TEXT (adb input text, with proper escaping)
- rn key KEY (adb input keyevent, e.g. TAB ENTER BACK DPAD_LEFT)
- rn tap X Y
- rn shot [out.png] (pull screencap)
- rn js 'code' (via HTTP control endpoint)
- rn dom (via HTTP)
- rn url (current page URL)
Symlink into /usr/local/bin/rn for global use.
close_reason: MVP shipped - browser + CLI working, tested Google search + CoreBalanceTraining login flow

title: Embed HTTP control server in browser
description:
Embed NanoHTTPD (or ktor) in browser app on localhost. Endpoints:
- POST /url {url} - navigate
- POST /js {code} - eval JS in webview, return result
- GET /dom - return rendered HTML
- GET /screenshot - return PNG
- POST /back - browser back
- GET /title - current page title
Exposed to Mac via adb forward tcp:PORT tcp:PORT.
close_reason: MVP shipped - browser + CLI working, tested Google search + CoreBalanceTraining login flow

title: Accessibility bridge service for cross-app control
description: Android AccessibilityService + HTTP API that can inject input into any app including TapLink. Requires user to enable in Settings→Accessibility once. API: /nodes (focus tree), /click {id}, /type {text}. Makes TapLink and other non-standard apps controllable too.

title: Stereo rendering for AR glasses (left+right eye duplicate)
description: The browser renders as a single 1280x480 view, but each eye only sees half of the frame so half the UI is visible per eye. TapLink duplicates its UI explicitly. Need a StereoLayout wrapper that (1) draws the child twice side-by-side via dispatchDraw, (2) remaps touch events from right half to left, (3) forces WebView to software layer so view.draw() captures its hardware-rendered content.
close_reason: StereoLayout implemented and verified - two identical UIs rendered side-by-side, one per eye, with touch remapping from right half to left. WebView forced to software layer so its rendering captures into view.draw().

title: Proper stereo that preserves hardware video (PixelCopy or VirtualDisplay)
description:
Current StereoLayout forces WebView to LAYER_TYPE_SOFTWARE so view.draw() captures its content for the right-eye mirror. That breaks hardware-accelerated video (YouTube renders pure black, Wistia mostly works because its HTML5 player is simpler).

Proper fix options:
1. Keep WebView in LAYER_TYPE_HARDWARE. Use PixelCopy.request(webview, bitmap, callback) on a render loop (~30-60 fps) to snapshot the real rendered output INCLUDING hardware layers. Draw that bitmap twice side-by-side in StereoLayout.
2. Render activity into a VirtualDisplay(640x480), put its Surface as SurfaceTexture into two SurfaceViews side-by-side on the main display.
3. Check RayNeo SDK for a system flag that tells the compositor to stereo-duplicate for non-stereo-aware apps.

PixelCopy is the most pragmatic — no SDK dependency, handles all WebView content, we control the framerate (can back off when page is static).

Until this ships, workaround is the stereo on/off toggle shipped in rn-<pending>.
close_reason: PixelCopy-based stereo working - YouTube video renders in both eyes while stereo stays permanently on. Removed toggle from dashboard (no longer needed).

title: Install and verify on glasses: Google, YouTube, CoreBalanceTraining login
description:
Install APK on RayNeo X3 Pro, test 3 real workflows:
1. google.com, type a query, open a result
2. Open a YouTube video and play it
3. Log into corebalancetraining.com and navigate to a specific post/video
All via rn CLI from the Mac.
close_reason: MVP shipped - browser + CLI working, tested Google search + CoreBalanceTraining login flow

title: RayNeo CLI-controllable browser & tools (epic)
description:
Build a CLI-controllable Android browser for RayNeo X3 glasses that works with adb input/keyevent, supports tab navigation, and has an HTTP control API. Follow-on: YouTube-focused viewer, accessibility bridge for controlling TapLink and other apps.

Goals:
- Google search
- Watch YouTube videos
- Log into corebalancetraining.com and navigate videos
- Full control via USB-ADB (primary) and RayNeo phone app (secondary)

Current blocker: TapLink (default browser on X3) has a custom on-screen keyboard that ignores adb input text, and no URL-scheme intent handler.

title: YouTube-focused viewer app (follow-on)
description: Separate app optimized for watching YouTube on AR glasses. Reuses WebView shell from rayneo-browser but defaults to youtube.com, supports keyboard shortcuts (space=play/pause, arrow keys seek), fullscreen by default. Low priority until main browser works.

title: Scaffold rayneo-browser Android app (Kotlin + WebView)
description: Minimal Android app: MainActivity with EditText URL bar + WebView + BackStack. Standard widgets only so adb input text/keyevent work. Intent-filter for VIEW/BROWSABLE with http/https schemes. Package com.jacobcole.rayneobrowser. Target API 33 (Android 13). Location: ~/code/RayNeoTools/browser/
close_reason: MVP shipped - browser + CLI working, tested Google search + CoreBalanceTraining login flow

title: Voice agent via temple-tap: CapSense gesture → mic → LLM → browser control
description:
Add a hands-free agent trigger. A tap, long-press, or gesture pattern on the glasses' temple-bar capacitive sensors opens a voice listener; the transcribed utterance is sent to an LLM agent (see rn-0gj) which drives the existing HTTP control API (url, js, tap-element, key, fullscreen, volume).

## Trigger
The X3 Pro exposes 7 CapSense channels as /dev/input/event6-event12 (KEY_SLEEP on ch6, KEY_WAKEUP on ch5, plain ABS on ch0-4). That's enough to detect:
- single tap (ch1 press+release within 300ms)
- long press (ch1 held >800ms)
- swipe-forward/back (sequential ch0→ch1→ch2 or reverse)

Two implementation paths for receiving the events:
1. Accessibility service (needs user toggle once) — simpler, works on any ROM
2. Direct evdev read from /dev/input/event* — needs INPUT permission; likely only works as a system app (unrealistic)

## Voice
Android SpeechRecognizer (on-device where available, falls back to Google). Push-to-talk while the trigger is held, or toggle start/stop on tap.

## Agent loop
Plug into rn-0gj: transcript + current URL + screenshot → LLM with tools bound to our HTTP endpoints. Stream partial transcript to a small overlay so user sees what's being heard. Agent runs until it emits 'done' or a timeout.

## Feedback
- Pre-gesture: nothing
- Listening: small 'voice' indicator bottom-left of each eye
- Thinking: indicator spins
- Speaking (TTS response) optional

## Dependencies
- Depends on rn-0gj (agent backend)
- Related to rn-48z (accessibility bridge — could host the CapSense listener)

title: System IME keyboard (and toolbar while IME is up) not stereo-rendered
description:
Current StereoLayout only duplicates Views inside our own Activity content. The on-screen keyboard (InputMethodService) runs in a separate system window layer and isn't affected — so when the keyboard pops up it renders once across the full 1280 width, only visible to one eye. User-reported: the toolbar/URL bar also loses stereo while the keyboard is up.

Options to investigate:
1. Build a custom IME (Input Method Service) that itself uses StereoLayout — our app could bundle this.
2. SurfaceControl / display mirror: intercept the system IME rendering and duplicate it.
3. Avoid IME entirely — drive text input exclusively via adb input text from the Mac CLI (current workaround).
4. RayNeo SDK check: there may be a system flag that forces all windows into stereo duplication.

Toolbar-stereo-breaks-when-IME-open: likely because the activity shrinks to accommodate the IME and the child's halved measurement no longer matches the visible region. Need to repro and fix the onMeasure behavior when WindowInsets adjust for IME.
